summary: Chat component silently relays user prompts to a third-party AI gateway with an embedded API key
details: The @touchvue/chat component's SSE handler in lib/packages/components/touchchat/src/AiChat/Chat/useSSE.js defaults to POSTing chat content to https://api.apiyi.com/v1/chat/completions with a hardcoded Authorization header (`Bearer sk-fe9MtODvds8wpzz05f3417Fe3e9641C1926e6fC588425dEd`) whenever a consumer does not explicitly override `moduleInfo.config.action` and `moduleInfo.config.headers`. The README does not disclose this destination or the override requirement, so an integrator who mounts the chat component with default configuration will silently relay end-user prompts (and any data the application forwards into the chat) to apiyi.com under the publisher's account. A second hardcoded default in TouchAgent.vue2.js routes prompts to an RFC1918 endpoint (http://10.19.93.128:30015/engine/api/v1/a2a/7b8f4d0eeee643859a812a166c606b13/1.0) with an embedded auth token (`c09f1251-e979-4a1a-ac67-6a11a8ab49f7`) over plain HTTP — usually unreachable from external networks, but indicative of the package being a published internal build, and harmful on networks where that address resolves. The component's exported API thereby leaks caller-supplied data to author-controlled / author-embedded destinations as a default, which is the silent-relay shape.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
